definitions: Organic compounds containing a carbonyl group (C=O) bonded to two other carbon atoms.
A class of organic compounds that includes acetone and other related substances, often produced during fat metabolism.
examples: The body produces ketones when it breaks down fat for energy.
High levels of ketones in the urine can indicate a medical condition.
Some diets aim to induce ketosis, a state where the body primarily burns ketones for fuel.
